description_long: This bungalow is a single-story residential building with a cross-gabled roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The exterior walls are finished in a light, weathered plaster or stucco, with some areas showing signs of age and wear. There are multiple doors and windows, with the main entrance located on the longer facade, and a secondary entrance on the shorter wing. The building sits on a rectangular plot with a small yard, and a paved road runs alongside one side. A tall, slender utility pole is positioned near the corner of the structure, and the rooflines create a distinct L-shaped footprint. The windows are arranged irregularly, contributing to an asymmetrical appearance.
description: A weathered, single-story bungalow with a cross-gabled red-tiled roof, light plaster walls, and irregularly placed windows and doors, set on a rectangular plot beside a road.
description_view_0: Front and side of the bungalow with the main entrance, visible cross-gabled roof, and a utility pole near the corner.
description_view_1: Rear and side view showing the L-shaped layout, secondary entrance, and more of the yard area.
description_view_2: Long side of the bungalow facing the road, with several windows and the full length of the roof visible.
description_view_3: Back and side view highlighting the extended wing and the road running parallel to the building.
